response:
# Perspective Audit — Product Carousel With Autoplay Animation

## Step 1 — Escalated perspectives confirmed

| Perspective | Incoming alarm | Deep review |
|---|---|---|
| 3. Vestibular & Motion Sensitivity | HIGH | Yes |
| 7. Cognitive & Neurodivergent | MEDIUM | Yes |

**Not reviewed (LOW / not escalated):** Magnification & Reflow, Environmental Contrast, Auditory Access, Keyboard & Motor Access, Screen Reader & Semantic Structure.

Scope discipline: the component's inline comments flag an absent `aria-live` region and other screen-reader concerns, but Screen Reader & Semantic Structure was **not** escalated, so it is out of scope for this run. Evidence over assertion — only the two escalated dimensions are audited below. Line citations reference the fixture code block for `ProductCarousel` (JSX + CSS).

---

## Perspective 3 — Vestibular & Motion Sensitivity (HIGH)

**WCAG (AA):** 2.2.2, 2.3.1, 2.5.4 · **Aspirational (AAA):** 2.3.3

### JTBD checklist walkthrough

| # | Checklist item | Result | Evidence |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Non-essential animations suppressed under `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce` | **FINDING** | No media query anywhere; comment at CSS line 101 states its absence. Badge pulse (132–138), inline slide transition (53–54), parallax (107–108) all run unconditionally. → V2 |
| 2 | Essential animations reduced to opacity/fade under reduced-motion | **FINDING** | The 0.6s slide `transform` transition (line 53) is the only arguably-functional animation; it has no reduced-motion fallback (should collapse to instant or short fade). → V2 |
| 3 | Auto-playing content >5s has visible pause/stop/hide control (2.2.2) | **FINDING** | `setInterval` advances every 3000ms indefinitely (lines 28–33). No pause/stop/hide control is rendered anywhere. Prev/next (68–81) change the slide but do **not** clear the timer. → V1 |
| 4 | No content flashes >3×/second (2.3.1) | **PASS** | `badgePulse` is `0.8s ... alternate` (line 132): a full bright→dim→bright cycle takes 1.6s ≈ 0.63 Hz, far below the 3/sec seizure threshold, and is a partial opacity dim (1→0.3) over a small element — not a general/red flash. Seizure red flag does **not** fire. |
| 5 | Device-motion functionality has UI alternative + can be disabled (2.5.4) | **N/A** | No shake/tilt/device-orientation behavior in this component. |
| 6 | Parallax scrolling removed or disabled under reduced-motion | **FINDING** | `data-parallax="true"` (line 47) + `background-attachment: fixed` with a background image (lines 107–108) produce scroll-coupled parallax, ungated by reduced-motion. → V3 (and swept into V2's fix) |
| 7 | Video auto-play disabled or user-controlled | **N/A** | No `<video>`/`<audio>` element present. |

### Red flag check

| Red flag | Fires? | Basis |
|---|---|---|
| Flashing content >3 flashes/second | No | 0.63 Hz partial dim (see item 4). |
| `prefers-reduced-motion` media query absent when animations exist | **YES → auto-CRITICAL** | Three animations exist; zero reduced-motion handling (CSS line 101 confirms). → V2 |
| No pause control on auto-playing carousel or video | **YES → auto-CRITICAL** | Auto-advancing carousel, no pause/stop/hide (lines 28–33). → V1 |

### Findings

**Finding:** The carousel auto-advances every 3 seconds indefinitely with no pause, stop, or hide control. `setInterval` (lines 28–33) drives motion the user cannot halt; the prev/next buttons re-index the slide but never call `clearInterval`, so the autoplay keeps firing and will fight a user who is manually navigating. For a person with a vestibular disorder, uncontrollable horizontal slide motion (plus the parallax and badge pulse in the same viewport) is not a mild annoyance — it can induce dizziness, nausea, or migraine and force them off the page. This is functionally blocking, not checklist-blocking.
**WCAG:** 2.2.2 Pause, Stop, Hide (Level A)
**Perspective:** Vestibular & Motion Sensitivity
**Severity:** CRITICAL (red flag: no pause control on auto-playing carousel)
**Route to:** UX Design (secondary: Front-End Dev)
**Evidence:** ProductCarousel JSX lines 28–33 (`setInterval`, 3000ms, no clear on interaction); no pause/stop control in the returned markup (lines 45–93).
**Fix:** Add a visible, keyboard-operable Pause/Play toggle as the first control in the carousel's focus/reading order. Pause the interval on hover, focus-within, and explicit toggle; do not auto-resume while the user is interacting. Respect `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce` by starting **paused** (see V2).

---

**Finding:** No `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` rule exists anywhere in the stylesheet (confirmed by the comment at CSS line 101), yet the component ships three concurrent animations: the 0.6s `transform` slide transition (inline, lines 51–54), the infinite `badgePulse` opacity animation (`0.8s ... infinite alternate`, lines 132–138), and the parallax background (lines 107–108). A user who has explicitly set the OS "reduce motion" preference receives the full motion payload regardless. The infinite badge pulse is also blinking content lasting well over 5 seconds with no mechanism to stop it.
**WCAG:** 2.2.2 Pause, Stop, Hide (Level A) — for the >5s blinking badge and the moving track; the reduced-motion gap is the mechanism. (Parallax's scroll-coupled motion additionally implicates 2.3.3 Animation from Interactions, AAA — see V3.)
**Perspective:** Vestibular & Motion Sensitivity
**Severity:** CRITICAL (red flag: `prefers-reduced-motion` media query absent when animations exist)
**Route to:** UX Design (secondary: Front-End Dev)
**Evidence:** CSS line 101 (documented absence of the media query); inline transition lines 51–54; `badgePulse` keyframes lines 132–138; parallax lines 107–108.
**Fix:** Wrap all non-essential motion in `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` and, under it: set the slide `transition` to `none` (or a ≤200ms opacity fade), set the badge `animation: none` (hold opacity 1), disable parallax (`background-attachment: scroll`), and keep autoplay stopped by default. Because the inline `transition` is set via the React `style` prop, it cannot be overridden by a stylesheet media query alone — read `matchMedia('(prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)')` in the component and omit the transition/interval when reduce is set.

---

**Finding:** The wrapper applies a parallax background (`data-parallax="true"`, line 47) via `background-attachment: fixed` (lines 107–108). As the user scrolls, the background moves at a different rate than the foreground — a classic vestibular trigger — with no reduced-motion guard.
**WCAG:** 2.3.3 Animation from Interactions (Level AAA)
**Perspective:** Vestibular & Motion Sensitivity
**Severity:** ENHANCEMENT (AAA criterion — not escalated to MAJOR/CRITICAL per calibration rules)
**Route to:** UX Design (secondary: Front-End Dev)
**Evidence:** JSX line 47 (`data-parallax="true"`); CSS lines 107–108 (`background-attachment: fixed` + `background-image`).
**Fix:** Same remediation as V2 — disable `background-attachment: fixed` under `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce`. Although 2.3.3 is AAA (hence ENHANCEMENT severity), the parallax is part of the same systemic reduced-motion gap that makes V2 CRITICAL and independently contributes to vestibular load; fixing V2 resolves it.

---

## Perspective 7 — Cognitive & Neurodivergent (MEDIUM)

**WCAG (AA):** 1.3.5, 2.2.1, 2.4.5, 3.2.1, 3.2.2, 3.2.3, 3.2.4, 3.2.6, 3.3.3, 3.3.4, 3.3.7, 3.3.8 · **Aspirational (AAA):** 3.1.3–3.1.5, 3.3.6

### JTBD checklist walkthrough

| Checklist item | Result | Evidence |
|---|---|---|
| Error messages suggest corrective action (3.3.3) | **N/A** | No forms, inputs, or validation in this component. |
| Multi-step form doesn't re-ask info (3.3.7) | **N/A** | No form. |
| Auth avoids transcription/memorization/puzzle (3.3.8) | **N/A** | No authentication. |
| Navigation same relative position across pages (3.2.3) | **N/A (unverifiable)** | Single component — cross-page consistency cannot be confirmed from source. |
| Same-function components consistently labeled (3.2.4) | **N/A (unverifiable)** | Cross-page scope not available. Within the component, prev/next are consistently labeled. |
| Help mechanism in same location every page (3.2.6) | **N/A** | No help mechanism present. |
| Focus does not auto-trigger context change (3.2.1) | **PASS** | Prev/next use `onClick` only (lines 70, 77); no `onFocus`/`onMouseOver` handlers that navigate. Focusing a control does not change context. |
| Changing a control value doesn't auto-submit (3.2.2) | **N/A** | No form controls. |
| Destructive actions have undo/confirmation (3.3.4) | **N/A** | No destructive actions. |
| More than one way to reach a page (2.4.5) | **N/A** | Component-level, not page-level. |
| Personal-data inputs have `autocomplete` (1.3.5) | **N/A** | No inputs. |
| Timeout warnings give ≥20s (2.2.1) | **FINDING (related)** | The 3s auto-advance imposes a ~3-second window to read each slide's name/price/badge with no way to extend or turn off. Primary citation is 2.2.2 (moving content); the tight reading window also implicates 2.2.1 Timing Adjustable. → C1 |
| Content avoids unexplained jargon / complex sentences | **PASS** | Copy is short product names + prices; no jargon barrier. |

**Neurodivergent-specific checks**

| Check | Result | Evidence |
|---|---|---|
| Predictable layout, no unprompted content shifts | **FINDING** | Content changes every 3s with no user action (lines 28–33) — unpredictable, self-initiated change. → C1 |
| Clear visual hierarchy (headings, spacing, grouping) | **PASS** | `<h2>` product name + `.price` per slide (lines 61–62); reasonable grouping. |
| No autoplay media or unprompted modals | **FINDING** | Autoplay carousel is present (lines 28–33). This check explicitly fails for cognitive/attention users. → C1 |
| Instructions don't rely on memory of prior steps | **N/A** | No multi-step instructions. |
| Continuous/looping animation not distracting attention | **FINDING** | Infinite `badgePulse` (lines 132–138) is persistent peripheral motion with no stop control. → C2 |

### Red flag check

Cognitive red flags — CAPTCHA/cognitive test, paste-disabled fields, multi-step re-collection, destructive action without confirmation — **none fire** (no auth, forms, or destructive actions). No auto-CRITICAL from this perspective, consistent with the incoming MEDIUM alarm.

### Findings

**Finding:** Auto-advancing content changes the visible product (name, price, badge) every 3 seconds with no pause or pace control (lines 28–33). A slower reader, or a person with a processing, attention, or working-memory difference, cannot reliably finish reading a slide or decide to act before it is replaced — and cannot slow it down. The change is self-initiated and therefore unpredictable, which compounds the barrier for users who rely on a stable, user-controlled layout.
**WCAG:** 2.2.2 Pause, Stop, Hide (Level A) — same root defect as V1, viewed through the cognitive lens; the required pause mechanism also satisfies 2.2.1 Timing Adjustable for the reading window.
**Perspective:** Cognitive & Neurodivergent
**Severity:** MAJOR (significant barrier — no workaround exists to control pace; no cognitive red flag fires, so not CRITICAL)
**Route to:** UX Design (secondary: Front-End Dev)
**Evidence:** ProductCarousel JSX lines 28–33 (3000ms auto-advance, no pause); no pace/stop control in markup (lines 45–93).
**Fix:** The pause/play control from V1 resolves this. Additionally, prefer starting the carousel paused, pausing on focus/hover, and never auto-resuming mid-interaction, so users set their own reading pace. Consider a longer interval as a secondary mitigation, but a user-operable pause is the actual requirement.

---

**Finding:** The product badge pulses continuously (`badgePulse 0.8s ease-in-out infinite alternate`, opacity 1↔0.3, lines 132–138) with no mechanism to stop it and no reduced-motion guard. Persistent peripheral motion is a well-documented attention barrier for ADHD, autistic, and other neurodivergent users — it repeatedly pulls the eye away from the content being read. In isolation a single pulsing badge trends toward MINOR, but here there is no way to stop it (so it fails the MINOR "workaround exists" definition) and it stacks with the auto-advancing carousel to make the viewport visually noisy.
**WCAG:** 2.2.2 Pause, Stop, Hide (Level A) — blinking content lasting >5s with no stop mechanism.
**Perspective:** Cognitive & Neurodivergent
**Severity:** MAJOR
**Route to:** UX Design (secondary: Front-End Dev)
**Evidence:** CSS `.product-badge` `animation` line 132; `@keyframes badgePulse` lines 135–138; no stop control; no reduced-motion guard (line 101).
**Fix:** Remove the infinite pulse or make it finite/user-suppressible; at minimum disable it under `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce` (covered by V2's fix). A static high-contrast badge communicates "NEW/SALE/HOT" without motion.

---

## Contract-compatible evidence blocks (new CRITICAL/MAJOR findings)

### A11y Evidence Finding
```
finding_id: carousel-autoplay-no-pause-control
fingerprint: a3f1c9d24b7e0182
source: perspective-audit Vestibular & Motion Sensitivity checklist
wcag_or_apg: WCAG 2.2 2.2.2 Pause, Stop, Hide (Level A); WAI-ARIA APG carousel/auto-rotation pattern (rotation control required)
section_508_fpc_context: In scope — WCAG 2.0 Level A, incorporated by Revised Section 508 (E205.4 / 508 Chapter 5)
severity: CRITICAL
perspective_alarms: vestibular_motion=HIGH, cognitive_neurodivergent=MEDIUM
evidence: ProductCarousel JSX lines 28-33 (setInterval 3000ms, no clearInterval on interaction); no pause/stop/hide control in returned markup lines 45-93
reproduction_steps: Render the component; observe the slide auto-advancing every 3s. Attempt to stop it — no control exists. Click Next (lines 68-74); the interval is not cleared, so autoplay continues to fire.
expected_behavior: Auto-updating content lasting >5s exposes a visible, keyboard-operable pause/stop/hide control and pauses on user interaction/focus.
actual_behavior: Carousel advances every 3s indefinitely with no way to pause; manual navigation does not stop the timer.
trend: new
```

### A11y Evidence Finding
```
finding_id: carousel-animations-no-reduced-motion
fingerprint: 7b2e4f9a1c8d3056
source: perspective-audit Vestibular & Motion Sensitivity checklist
wcag_or_apg: WCAG 2.2 2.2.2 Pause, Stop, Hide (Level A); related 2.3.3 Animation from Interactions (Level AAA, ENHANCEMENT)
section_508_fpc_context: 2.2.2 in scope (WCAG 2.0 Level A, Revised Section 508); 2.3.3 not in scope (WCAG 2.1 AAA)
severity: CRITICAL
perspective_alarms: vestibular_motion=HIGH, cognitive_neurodivergent=MEDIUM
evidence: CSS line 101 (documented absence of @media prefers-reduced-motion); inline transition lines 51-54; @keyframes badgePulse lines 132-138; parallax background-attachment: fixed lines 107-108
reproduction_steps: Enable OS "reduce motion". Load the component. Observe that the slide transition, infinite badge pulse, and scroll parallax all still animate. Grep stylesheet for prefers-reduced-motion — none found.
expected_behavior: Under prefers-reduced-motion: reduce, non-essential motion (slide transition, badge pulse, parallax) is removed or reduced to a short opacity fade, and autoplay defaults to paused.
actual_behavior: No reduced-motion handling exists; all animation runs regardless of the user's OS preference. The inline React style transition cannot be overridden by a stylesheet media query alone.
trend: new
```

### A11y Evidence Finding
```
finding_id: carousel-autoplay-cognitive-pace-control
fingerprint: c4d8a1f6e2b90734
source: perspective-audit Cognitive & Neurodivergent checklist
wcag_or_apg: WCAG 2.2 2.2.2 Pause, Stop, Hide (Level A); related 2.2.1 Timing Adjustable (Level A)
section_508_fpc_context: In scope — WCAG 2.0 Level A, incorporated by Revised Section 508
severity: MAJOR
perspective_alarms: cognitive_neurodivergent=MEDIUM, vestibular_motion=HIGH
evidence: ProductCarousel JSX lines 28-33 (content changes every 3s); no pace/pause control lines 45-93
reproduction_steps: Load the component; attempt to read a slide's name and price and then act on it before it changes. Content is replaced after ~3s with no way to slow or stop it.
expected_behavior: User can pause or control the pace of auto-changing content to read and act at their own speed.
actual_behavior: Content auto-advances every 3s with no pause; slower readers and users with attention/working-memory differences cannot finish before it changes.
trend: new
```

### A11y Evidence Finding
```
finding_id: badge-pulse-continuous-distraction
fingerprint: 9e1a7c3f5b2d84a0
source: perspective-audit Cognitive & Neurodivergent checklist
wcag_or_apg: WCAG 2.2 2.2.2 Pause, Stop, Hide (Level A) — blinking content >5s
section_508_fpc_context: In scope — WCAG 2.0 Level A, incorporated by Revised Section 508
severity: MAJOR
perspective_alarms: cognitive_neurodivergent=MEDIUM, vestibular_motion=HIGH
evidence: CSS .product-badge animation line 132; @keyframes badgePulse lines 135-138; no stop control; no reduced-motion guard (line 101)
reproduction_steps: Load the component; observe the badge pulsing opacity 1 to 0.3 continuously with no control to stop it.
expected_behavior: Continuous non-essential motion can be stopped or suppressed and does not persist in the periphery without user control.
actual_behavior: Badge pulses indefinitely (~0.63 Hz) with no stop mechanism and no reduced-motion guard.
trend: new
```

---

## Perspective Audit Summary

**Component/artifact reviewed:** Product Carousel With Autoplay Animation (`ProductCarousel` fixture — JSX + CSS)
**Perspectives escalated for deep review:** Vestibular & Motion Sensitivity (HIGH), Cognitive & Neurodivergent (MEDIUM)
**Perspectives at LOW (not reviewed):** Magnification & Reflow, Environmental Contrast, Auditory Access, Keyboard & Motor Access, Screen Reader & Semantic Structure

### Findings by Severity

| Severity | Count |
|---|---|
| CRITICAL | 2 |
| MAJOR | 2 |
| MINOR | 0 |
| ENHANCEMENT | 1 |

### Perspectives That Passed

None — both escalated perspectives produced findings. (The 2.3.1 flash-rate red flag was checked and correctly did **not** fire: the badge pulse runs at ~0.63 Hz, well under the 3-flashes/second seizure threshold.)

### Perspectives With Findings — Grouped by ARRM Role

**UX Design** (motion policy / interaction decisions — primary owner)
- Auto-advancing carousel with no pause/stop/hide control — Vestibular & Motion — CRITICAL — 2.2.2 (V1)
- No `prefers-reduced-motion` guard on any animation — Vestibular & Motion — CRITICAL — 2.2.2 (V2)
- Auto-advance gives no reading-pace control — Cognitive & Neurodivergent — MAJOR — 2.2.2 (C1)
- Continuous infinite badge pulse, no stop control — Cognitive & Neurodivergent — MAJOR — 2.2.2 (C2)
- Parallax background not disabled under reduced-motion — Vestibular & Motion — ENHANCEMENT — 2.3.3 AAA (V3)

**Front-End Dev** (secondary — implements every fix above)
- All five findings require FE implementation: a pause/play toggle with interval control (V1/C1), a `prefers-reduced-motion` branch in both CSS and the React component to strip the inline `transition`/`setInterval` (V2/C2/V3).

**Visual Design** — None
**Content Author** — None

### Open Questions Requiring Human Verification

- **Cross-page consistency (3.2.3 / 3.2.4 / 3.2.6):** navigation position, consistent component identification, and help-location consistency cannot be confirmed from a single isolated component — verify at the page/site level if this carousel ships inside a larger template. [Human verification required — cannot be confirmed from source]
- No content-level (caption/transcript/alt-quality/reading-level) findings arose within the two escalated perspectives; all findings are code-level and enforceable from source.

### Recommended Next Step

**BLOCK** — 2 CRITICAL findings present (V1 autoplay with no pause control; V2 total absence of `prefers-reduced-motion` support). Do not ship until both are resolved. The two MAJOR cognitive findings (C1, C2) share the same fixes and will be resolved by the same work. Re-run `a11y-test` after remediation to verify: (a) a keyboard-operable pause control exists and stops the interval, and (b) with OS reduce-motion enabled, the slide transition, badge pulse, and parallax are all suppressed and autoplay defaults to paused.

_benchmark.mechanism: Claude Code Agent(general-purpose, model=opus), background, one per fixture
_benchmark.protocol_note: prompts composed identically to run_cloud_benchmark.py build_escalation_prompt EXCEPT fixture truncated before '## Accessibility Issues' (answer key withheld — prior lanes were non-blind)
